source: iConnections Global Alts 2026 — AI, Tesla, defense, energy — what comes next
summary: Anthropic is ~4x more capital efficient than OpenAI and growing fast with low cash burn
summary_extended: Baker argues that Anthropic is roughly four times more capital efficient than OpenAI, growing rapidly while consuming relatively little cash, making it a structurally superior AI lab from an investment standpoint. He supports this claim by noting that Anthropic's models can complete comparable-quality tasks using approximately half the tokens of competitors such as Google, which translates directly into lower inference costs and higher margins at scale. This efficiency advantage compounds over time: as AI workloads grow, Anthropic's cost structure allows it to reinvest less capital to maintain competitive parity, widening the gap versus more capital-intensive rivals. The investment implication is primarily relevant to private market investors, as Anthropic is not publicly listed; there is no directly actionable US-listed equity play implied by this specific thesis. Baker presents this as a high-conviction factual observation rather than speculation, grounding it in a specific ratio and a concrete technical mechanism (token efficiency).
detail: Baker states Anthropic is roughly four times more capital efficient than OpenAI, growing rapidly while not burning much cash. Their models can perform comparable quality tasks using half the tokens of competitors like Google, making them structurally advantaged on cost.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a direct factual claim with specific ratio ('roughly four times more capital efficient'), not hedged as speculation
quote_fragment: anthropic is roughly four times more capital efficient than OpenAI
